channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills 2 Palestinians in the occupied West Bank, officials say
item.description: Israel killed two Palestinians in the northern West Bank overnight and into Wednesday, the occupied territory's Heath Ministry said, as the Israeli military focuses on a major crackdown there during the ceasefire in Gaza. There was no immediate comment from the Israel's military.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Former Hamas hostage details horrors of captivity, credits kidnapped IDF soldier with saving her life
item.description: Former hostage Amit Soussana, who was the first Israeli woman to speak about being sexual assaulted while in Hamas captivity, says kidnapped IDF soldier Liri Albag saved her life.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: International Criminal Court has no trial scheduled as Trump piles on pressure
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — For a few hours last week, the International Criminal Court looked poised to take a Libyan warlord into custody. Instead, member state Italy sent the head of a notorious network of detention centers back home.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Year of the Snake is underway with Lunar New Year festivities around the world
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Firecrackers popped, incense was offered at temples and dancers and drummers paraded Wednesday in Asia and farther afield as millions around the world celebrated the Lunar New Year.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tiger poachers use fishing boats to smuggle body parts out of Malaysia, study shows
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Commercial fishing fleets have been playing a key role in trafficking parts of tigers poached in Malaysia, according to research released Wednesday that could help enforcement efforts to save the critically endangered cat.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australian police link stash of explosives and list of Jewish targets to an antisemitic plot
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australian police believe explosives found on Sydney’s outskirts were evidence of a deadly escalation in a campaign of antisemitic arson and graffiti crimes that has been waged in major cities for months, officials said on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Police say at least 30 people have died in a stampede at the massive Maha Kumbh festival in India
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — At least 30 people were killed and many more injured in a stampede at the world’s largest religious gathering early Wednesday, police said, as millions of pilgrims rushed to dip in sacred waters during the Maha Kumbh festival in northern India.
item.dc:creator: RAJESH KUMAR SINGH and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Western officials warn Ukraine over weapons procurement dispute
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Western officials have warned Ukraine that an escalating rift between the defense minister and procurement chief may jeopardize trust in the country and urged the government to resolve the situation quickly so as not to disrupt weapons supplies.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: German parliament backs election front-runner's call for tough border rules with far-right support
item.description: Germany’s parliament has narrowly approved a call by Chancellor Olaf Scholz’s main challenger to turn back many more migrants at the country’s borders, with apparent backing from a far-right party.
item.dc:creator: Geir Moulson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US watchdog launches database on Catholic priests accused of sex abuse of minors in the Philippines
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A United States-based watchdog on Wednesday launched an online database on more than 80 Roman Catholic priests who have been accused of sexually abusing minors in the Philippines and said the silence of Filipino bishops on the crimes amounted to a cover-up.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An unlikely symbol of cooperation, the barn owl brings Arab and Israeli scientists together
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — It soars over the borders and divisions of the war-torn Middle East, bringing Arab and Israeli academics together. An unlikely symbol of cooperation, the barn owl is now getting global attention.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels capture more cities as some Congolese forces lay down their weapons
item.description: Rebels backed by Rwanda captured more towns in eastern Congo as fighters moved beyond the key city of Goma in an apparent attempt to expand their control in the conflict-battered region.
item.dc:creator: Chinedu Asadu And Justin Kabumba, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Brazil to set up deportee reception center after contentious flight from US
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — The Brazilian government Tuesday said it will create a reception center for deported migrants from the United States following controversy over conditions on a recent deportation flight.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES and DAVID BILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Denmark to increase military spending in North Atlantic by $2 billion amid row with Trump over Greenland
item.description: Denmark is increasing military spending in the North Atlantic amid President Donald Trump’s bid to have Greenland sold or ceded to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congo tries to slow the assault of Rwanda-backed rebels amid reports of dead bodies in the streets
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Congolese security forces on Tuesday tried to slow the advance of Rwanda-backed M23 rebels who say they have captured Goma after entering eastern Congo's largest city, as U.N. officials reported violence, looting and bodies in the streets.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombian customs official pleads guilty in money laundering case tied to DEA misconduct
item.description: TAMPA, Florida (AP) — A Colombian customs worker admitted his role Tuesday in taking bribes and funneling more than $1 million in drug proceeds in a case that threatened to expose dirty dealings between U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration agents and their informants.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN and JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The discovery of brutal mass graves in Syria reveals Assad's legacy of horror
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The charred remains of at least 26 victims of the Bashar Assad government were located Tuesday by Syrian civil defense workers in two separate basements in rural Damascus.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM AND SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serbia's prime minister resigns as anti-corruption protests sparked by deadly collapse swell
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Serbia’s populist prime minister, Milos Vucevic, resigned Tuesday in an attempt to calm political tensions stoked by weeks of massive anti-corruption protests that started after the deadly collapse of a concrete overhang at a recently renovated train station.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Hamas hostage Naama Levy breaks silence in first message since her release
item.description: Naama Levy, one of the four IDF soldiers released from Hamas captivity on Saturday, writes a message of gratitude in her first statement since arriving home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doomsday clock ticks closer to catastrophe in dire global warning
item.description: The world moved yet closer to global catastrophe in 2024, with the hands of the Doomsday Clock ticking one second closer to midnight, the shortest time to zero hour in its 75-year history.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Serbian farmers join striking university students' 24-hour traffic blockade in Belgrade
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Serbia's striking university students on Monday launched a 24-hour blockade of a key traffic intersection in the capital, Belgrade, stepping up pressure on the populist authorities over a deadly canopy collapse in November that killed 15 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU official: Europe 'not negotiating' on Greenland
item.description: European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said Monday that Europe is “not negotiating” with the United States over control of Greenland, as President Trump continues to insist that acquiring the self-governing territory of Denmark is necessary for American national security.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Palestinians stream back to northern Gaza after Israel lifts closure
item.description: Thousands of Palestinians are returning to the northern Gaza Strip Monday as part of the cease-fire and hostage release deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House: Colombia to take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth over immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: 22 reported killed in Lebanon before agreement to extend deadline for Israeli forces to withdraw
item.description: MAYS AL-JABAL, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli forces in southern Lebanon on Sunday opened fire on protesters demanding their withdrawal in line with a ceasefire agreement, killing at least 22 and injuring 124, Lebanese health officials reported.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Colombia to send presidential plane to Honduras to pick up migrants from US flights
item.description: President Gustavo Petro has arranged for the presidential plane to facilitate the "dignified return of Colombian nationals "
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: 13 UN peacekeepers, allied soldiers dead in Congo as M23 rebels make gains in key city
item.description: The Rwanda-backed rebel group M23 is battling Congolese government forces in Congo's key city of Goma, causing the international airport there to be evacuated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In ‘Hail Mary’ move, ICC prosecutor files surprise application as Senate set to vote on sanctioning court
item.description: As the ICC prosecutor files applications for warrants against the Taliban, the Senate is set to vote on sanctions in bill that would trigger action among other things if US citizens, allies are prosecuted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainian human rights lawyer, Nobel Prize winner urges Trump to show strength and end the war
item.description: Ukrainian human rights attorney and Nobel Peace Prize winner Oleksandra Matviichuk urges President Trump to show strength as he tries to bring the Russia-Ukraine war to an end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants Egypt and Jordan to take in Palestinians from Gaza. Here's why they are likely to refuse
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's suggestion that Egypt and Jordan take in Palestinians from the war-ravaged Gaza Strip is likely to be met with a hard “no" from the two U.S. allies and the Palestinians themselves, who fear Israel would never allow them to return.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli victims of terror concerned with murderers' release from prison, relieved 7 hostages back home
item.description: The release of hostages in exchange for convicted terrorists under Israel’s ceasefire deal with Hamas stirs profound emotions, reigniting debates over justice, security and national unity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas frees 4 female Israeli soldiers in exchange for 200 Palestinian prisoners as ceasefire holds
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Four female Israeli soldiers who were taken in the attack that sparked the war in Gaza returned to Israel on Saturday after Hamas militants paraded them before a crowd of thousands in Gaza City and handed them over to the Red Cross.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, SAM MEDNICK, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England sentenced to over 50 years
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teenager who stabbed three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England was sentenced Thursday to more than 50 years in prison for what a judge called “the most extreme, shocking and exceptionally serious crime.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, WGN 9
